maps.surface: A4 close-path telemetry (nv-tb-egpu-close.c) vs A7 F40b shutdown wrapper (nv.c) — WPR2 reconciliation for H-SH6 / SH-1

metrics.name: WPR2 (BAR0+0x88a828, bits 31:4 via TB_EGPU_PCIE_WPR2_VAL_MASK) before / during-hang / after
metrics.what_it_tells_us: The H-SH6 discriminator. 'before' = WPR2 up (0x07f4a000) confirms GSP-resident pre-teardown. 'during-hang' (sampled while rm_shutdown_adapter worker is mid-200ms+ timeout) is the NEW reading no prior experiment has: if WPR2 is ALREADY 0 during the hang -> the GSP-shutdown handshake the chip is stuck on is POST-WPR2-clear (late stall); if WPR2 still up during the hang -> stuck BEFORE clearing WPR2 (early stall). 'after' (A4 post-shutdown line) is already known to read 0 on the non-persistent path.
metrics.how_to_read: in-driver primitive tb_egpu_pcie_read_wpr2(bar0_phys,&raw) (nv-tb-egpu-pcie.c:48) — already wired into A4's tb_egpu_close_diag_pdev (nv-tb-egpu-close.c:121). Passive equivalent for the 'during' sample: setpci/devmem READ of phys 0x80000000+0x88a828 = 0x8088a828 on a NON-wedged chip only; on the live healthy aorus.21 BAR0 phys is 0x80000000 (confirmed /sys/.../0000:04:00.0/resource).
metrics.when_to_sample: before (at A7 'scheduled' log), during-hang (sample at ~100ms and ~250ms after 'scheduled', i.e. inside the 10s SH-1 budget while worker pegs a core), after (A4 post-shutdown line). During-hang is the load-bearing NEW sample.
metrics.safety_note: During-hang read goes to the SAME BAR0 the hung worker is touching. If the chip has actually gone MMIO-dead (H-SH3) a config/BAR0 read could itself stall the reader. Mitigation: read WPR2 from the syscall thread BEFORE scheduling and AFTER timeout via A4 (those are proven safe — they fire on every live close), and obtain the during-hang value from the worker side only via the instrumented SH-2 path, OR accept the in-driver A4 read is issued by a thread that is NOT the blocked worker. Prefer bridge-side liveness (PMC_BOOT_0 readability) to gate whether a during-hang BAR0 read is safe.

metrics.name: PMC_BOOT_0 (BAR0+0x0) readability before/during/after
metrics.what_it_tells_us: Alive-check: distinguishes H-SH1 (chip alive, answering MMIO, bit never flips -> PMC_BOOT_0 keeps returning 0x1b2000a1 throughout the hang) from H-SH3 (single non-returning MMIO read, chip MMIO-dead -> PMC_BOOT_0 read returns 0xFFFFFFFF or itself stalls). On the live driver PMC_BOOT_0=0x1b2000a1 at every A4 site including post-shutdown AFTER the A7 timeout — strong prior evidence for H-SH1 (chip alive across the hang).
metrics.how_to_read: A4 already reads it: ioread32(ioremap(bar0_phys,PAGE_SIZE)) in tb_egpu_close_diag_pdev (nv-tb-egpu-close.c:112-117). Logged as PMC_BOOT_0=0x%08x on every CLOSE line.
metrics.when_to_sample: before (A7 scheduled), during-hang (gates whether other during-hang reads are safe), after (A4 post-shutdown).
metrics.safety_note: Same BAR0-on-hung-device caveat as WPR2. Use as the FIRST during-hang read; if it returns 0xFFFFFFFF or stalls, do NOT issue further during-hang BAR0 reads (chip went dead — that itself is the H-SH3 answer).

metrics.name: Upstream-bridge AER UESta + completion-timeout TLP Header-Log address
metrics.what_it_tells_us: Whether the hang produces a PCIe Completion Timeout at all, and if so the TLP address pins WHICH chip register rm_shutdown_adapter was waiting on (H-SH4). Note: the A7 log line says 'declaring GPU lost (detector_class=3 DETECTOR_AER_FATAL)' but that is a PLACEHOLDER label set unconditionally on timeout — it is NOT evidence an AER actually fired. The bridge AER status is the independent check. If bridge AER UESta stays 0x0 across the whole hang, the worker is BUSY-POLLING a register that IS completing (H-SH1, no CTO), not blocked on a non-returning TLP (H-SH3).
metrics.how_to_read: passive setpci on the BRIDGE not the device: setpci -s 03:00.0 ECAP_AER+0x10.L (UESta) and +0x1c..+0x28 (Header Log) on 0000:03:00.0 (the GPU's immediate upstream port, driver=pcieport, confirmed in topology). Repeat on 02:00.0 / 00:07.0 if 03:00.0 shows nothing.
metrics.when_to_sample: during-hang (continuous poll of the bridge every ~250ms) and after.
metrics.safety_note: Reading the BRIDGE config space is safe even when the leaf device is wedged — this is the project's standard 'read the bridge not the hung device' discipline.

metrics.name: Worker thread CPU-burn (utime+stime delta) and scheduling state (R vs D)
metrics.what_it_tells_us: The poll-vs-block discriminator (H-SH1 vs H-SH3). R-state with rising utime/stime = busy-poll loop (chip alive, bit never flips). D-state with flat utime = single blocked uninterruptible MMIO read. User-empirical already says one core pegs => leans H-SH1; this quantifies it.
metrics.how_to_read: identify the nv_f40b_shutdown_worker kthread on system_long_wq (ps -eLo pid,tid,stat,comm,wchan | grep -i kworker around the fire), sample /proc/<tid>/stat fields 14/15 (utime/stime) and field 3 (state) at ~250ms cadence during the 10s window.
metrics.when_to_sample: during-hang, ~250ms cadence across the 10s SH-1 budget.

metrics.name: rm_disable_adapter vs rm_shutdown_adapter latency (journal-timestamp ballpark)
metrics.what_it_tells_us: Confirms the asymmetry that is the sharpest lead: rm_disable completes within budget, rm_shutdown times out — so the hang is specific to the final GSP-shutdown step, not teardown generally. On the live driver both fire back-to-back at 13:01:02 (disable 'completed within budget', shutdown 'timed out after 200 ms') confirming the asymmetry already.
metrics.how_to_read: journal timestamps between 'X scheduled to bounded worker' and 'X completed within budget'/'X timed out after N ms' lines (NVRM: tb_egpu [F40b]: ... in nv_f40b_shutdown_bounded, nv.c:2241-2261).
metrics.when_to_sample: during (both calls, every teardown).

metrics.name: Completes-at-T or never, at 10s budget (NVreg_TbEgpuShutdownTimeoutMs=10000)
metrics.what_it_tells_us: Resolves H-SH2 (is 200ms simply too tight?). If rm_shutdown_adapter 'completed within budget' appears at e.g. 1-3s -> the call DOES finish, A7's 200ms is too aggressive, and the teardown is slow-but-graceful (WPR2 would clear via the real path). If it 'timed out after 10000 ms' -> genuinely non-terminating, the disease is real, A7 containment is necessary.
metrics.how_to_read: same NVRM [F40b] log line as latency metric, but with the 10s module-param override (the ONLY variable SH-1 changes vs production 200ms).
metrics.when_to_sample: during, single close-path teardown, n>=3.
metrics.safety_note: Per ledger thermal budget: cap at 10s (not 30s) — the pegged core spikes NUC package temp (project_h21_cpu_thermals).

metrics.name: tb_egpu_f40b_fires sysfs counter cross-check
metrics.what_it_tells_us: Confirms the teardown timeout is counted and that state is NOT stranded at lost-temporary (A8 v2.1 fix). On the live HEALTHY driver right now: tb_egpu_f40b_fires=1, tb_egpu_state=healthy — i.e. the one bring-up close-path rm_shutdown timeout was counted, yet state correctly stayed healthy. This is direct confirmation the SHUTDOWN-arm fire is a benign-per-bringup event, distinct from the OPEN-arm wedge.
metrics.how_to_read: cat /sys/bus/pci/devices/0000:04:00.0/tb_egpu_f40b_fires and tb_egpu_state (A8 v2.1 sysfs_create_group surface, confirmed present live).
metrics.when_to_sample: before and after each teardown (delta should be +1 per rm_shutdown timeout).

metrics.name: PCIe link state (current_link_speed/width, LinkActive) on bridge during hang
metrics.what_it_tells_us: Confirms the TB tunnel + link stays up during the hang (so the hang is a chip-internal handshake, not a link drop). If link drops -> different failure class.
metrics.how_to_read: cat /sys/bus/pci/devices/0000:03:00.0/current_link_speed and current_link_width (bridge-side, safe).
metrics.when_to_sample: during-hang.

key_findings: RECONCILED — there is no contradiction; A4 and A7 observe DIFFERENT POINTS on the SAME timeline, and the two 'WPR2->0 clean close' vs 'WPR2 up' observations were on DIFFERENT close paths (non-persistent vs persistent). I confirmed this LIVE on aorus.21 in the 13:01:02-13:01:04 journal window.

ORDERING (from nv.c source): A7's wrapper lives INSIDE nv_shutdown_adapter (nv.c:2290). The sequence is: nv_stop_device (non-persistent branch, nv.c:2390-2394) -> nv_shutdown_adapter -> [A7] nv_f40b_shutdown_bounded(rm_disable_adapter) [completes] -> host-side teardown -> [A7] nv_f40b_shutdown_bounded(rm_shutdown_adapter) [TIMES OUT at 200ms, returns] -> nv_shutdown_adapter RETURNS -> back in nv_stop_device, A4's tb_egpu_close_diag(nvl,'post-shutdown',...) fires at nv.c:2402. So A4 post-shutdown fires STRICTLY AFTER nv_shutdown_adapter returns, which is STRICTLY AFTER A7's rm_shutdown_adapter timeout. A4 is downstream of A7 on the call stack.

ANSWER TO Q1: YES — 'nv_shutdown_adapter completes' is true-VIA-A7-TIMEOUT, not via graceful GSP completion. A7 forces nv_shutdown_adapter to return after the bounded 200ms (the worker that actually holds the chip-touching MMIO is leaked, not joined). A4's post-shutdown line then fires ~1s later. Live proof: 13:01:02 'rm_shutdown_adapter timed out after 200 ms', then 13:01:03 'post-shutdown ... WPR2=0x00000000'. A4 'completing' was always downstream of (and gated by) A7's timeout on the non-persistent path — A4 never witnessed graceful rm_shutdown completion; it witnessed A7's bounded return.

ANSWER TO Q2: The F40 catalog's two WPR2 claims are on TWO DIFFERENT close paths and the doc conflates them. (a) The 'WPR2 STAYS at 0x07f4a000' claim (F40 Run B / Assertion-shape line ~454, and PINPOINT-1's pre-stop) is the PERSISTENCE-ENGAGED path: nv_stop_device takes the rm_disable_adapter branch (nv.c:2386-2388), NEVER calls nv_shutdown_adapter, so no A7 shutdown-wrapper fires and GSP stays resident -> WPR2 up. (b) The 'WPR2 -> 0' clean-close claim (F40 PINPOINT-1 post-shutdown line ~474, '0x00000000 wpr2_up:no') is the NON-PERSISTENT path that DOES run nv_shutdown_adapter. Both are reproduced LIVE: persistent closes at 13:01:04/13:02:30 show post-shutdown WPR2=0x07f4a000 up; the one non-persistent close at 13:01:03 shows post-shutdown WPR2=0x00000000. The A7 timeout ONLY occurs on path (b). So A7's 'times out every healthy rmmod' and A4's 'WPR2->0 clean close' BOTH describe path (b) and are the SAME event viewed at different stack depths.

ANSWER TO Q3 — THE KEY MECHANISTIC INSIGHT: WPR2 reads 0 at the A4 post-shutdown site EVEN THOUGH rm_shutdown_adapter TIMED OUT and never gracefully finished. This means WPR2-clear happens EARLY in rm_shutdown_adapter's work, BEFORE the chip-touching MMIO that A7's worker is still hung on. The leaked worker is stuck LATE (post-WPR2-clear) on some other GSP-shutdown-coordination / register-flush MMIO. Therefore: (i) WPR2=0 is NOT evidence of graceful completion — it is evidence the teardown got PAST the WPR2-tearing step before stalling; (ii) the disease A7 contains is a LATE-stage stall, after the WPR2 invariant is already torn down. This directly supports H-SH1 (chip alive, answering reads, a later handshake bit never flips) over a wholesale early MMIO death.

ANSWER TO Q4 — CANDIDATE MODELS and what SH-1's during-hang WPR2 disambiguates: MODEL A (LATE-STALL, leading, consistent with all live data): WPR2 sequence = before:UP / during-hang:0 / after(A4):0. WPR2 clears early inside rm_shutdown, worker then hangs on a later post-WPR2 chip handshake; A7 returns at 200ms; A4 logs WPR2=0. The 'after' reading being 0 is already confirmed live; the NEW during-hang sample being 0 CONFIRMS Model A. MODEL B (EARLY-STALL, less likely): if rm_shutdown hangs BEFORE it clears WPR2, then during-hang:UP and the A4 post-shutdown WPR2=0 could only mean the LEAKED worker eventually cleared WPR2 between the timeout and the A4 read (a race) — testable because A4 fires only ~1s after the timeout; if during-hang shows WPR2 still UP but A4 shows 0, the worker cleared it in that ~1s gap (worker IS making progress, just slow -> shifts weight toward H-SH2 'budget too tight'). MODEL C (MMIO-DEAD, H-SH3): chip stops answering during the hang -> PMC_BOOT_0 during-hang returns 0xFFFFFFFF and WPR2 during-hang is unreadable/garbage; A4's post-shutdown PMC_BOOT_0 would then NOT be 0x1b2000a1 — but live data shows it IS 0x1b2000a1 at post-shutdown, making Model C already unlikely. SH-1's during-hang {PMC_BOOT_0, WPR2} pair is the single measurement that selects among A/B/C.

DISAMBIGUATING MEASUREMENT (the thing SH-1 must add): the DURING-HANG WPR2+PMC_BOOT_0 pair, sampled while the rm_shutdown_adapter worker is mid-timeout. Every other reading (before, after) is already captured by A4 on every live close. The before/after WPR2 transition (UP->0) is NOT discriminating on its own (it is identical under Models A and B). Only the during-hang value separates them: during=0 -> Model A (late stall, H-SH1); during=UP-then-A4=0 -> Model B (slow-but-progressing, H-SH2); during=unreadable/PMC=0xFFFFFFFF -> Model C (H-SH3).

SAFETY CAVEAT for SH-1: the during-hang WPR2/PMC_BOOT_0 reads target the SAME BAR0 the hung worker touches. Gate them: issue PMC_BOOT_0 first; if it returns 0xFFFFFFFF or the reader itself stalls, STOP (that is already the Model-C answer) and do not issue the WPR2 read. Bridge-side AER + link-state reads (on 03:00.0) are unconditionally safe and should run continuously regardless. Per the ledger, cap the shutdown timeout experiment at 10s, not 30s, for NUC thermal reasons.

DOC-CORRECTNESS NOTE for the parent: F40's historical body (and its 'Assertion shape' Run-A/Run-B section) treats 'A4 post-shutdown fires with WPR2 cleared' as proof the destructive teardown COMPLETED ('The destructive teardown is not the problem'). Post-A7, that inference is unsafe: A4 post-shutdown firing only proves nv_shutdown_adapter RETURNED, which A7 now guarantees at 200ms regardless of whether rm_shutdown_adapter actually finished. The F40 morning conclusion 'teardown completes, wedge is in the next OPEN' was reasoning from a pre-A7 driver where nv_shutdown_adapter returning DID imply completion; that implication no longer holds and the F40 catalog's shutdown-arm section already acknowledges this, but the historical 'destructive teardown is not the problem' lines should be read as superseded.
existing_in_driver_primitives: nv-tb-egpu-close.c:tb_egpu_close_diag_pdev (reads PMC_BOOT_0 via ioremap+ioread32 and WPR2 via tb_egpu_pcie_read_wpr2; logs both + wpr2_up verdict on every LAST-CLOSE)
nv-tb-egpu-close.c:tb_egpu_close_diag (RM-side wrapper; the 4 nv.c call sites close-entry/pre-stop/post-shutdown/close-exit)
nv-tb-egpu-pcie.c:tb_egpu_pcie_read_wpr2 (raw WPR2 read at BAR0+0x88a828, mask 0xfffffff0; shared A1/A3/A4 helper)
nv.c:nv_f40b_shutdown_bounded (A7 wrapper; emits 'scheduled'/'completed within budget'/'timed out after N ms' NVRM lines — the latency + completes-or-never source)
nv.c:nv_shutdown_adapter (A7 wrap sites for rm_disable_adapter @2298 and rm_shutdown_adapter @2348; A4 post-shutdown fires after this returns @2402)
nv.c:nv_stop_device (the persistent rm_disable_adapter branch @2388 vs non-persistent nv_shutdown_adapter branch @2393 — the fork that determines whether A7 fires and whether WPR2 clears)
nv-tb-egpu-metrics.c (A8 v2.1): nv_tb_egpu_f40b_fired_teardown + tb_egpu_f40b_fires/tb_egpu_state sysfs (counts the teardown timeout without stranding state)

sh1_capture_list.metric: Live baseline snapshot: A7 timeout=200ms (prod), recover/qwd enable flags, driver version string
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: cat /sys/module/nvidia/version (confirm aorus.NN actually loaded — live is 595.71.05-aorus.17, task says .21); cat /sys/bus/pci/devices/0000:04:00.0/tb_egpu_f40b_fires tb_egpu_state; grep NVreg_TbEgpu* in modprobe.d
sh1_capture_list.rationale: Establishes the before-deltas every later sample measures against; confirms which driver/timeout is live so the F40b worker symbols and 200ms->10s override apply.

sh1_capture_list.metric: DPC capability presence on whole chain (NEGATIVE/dead-metric — record absent, do not poll)
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: lspci -s 00:07.0 -vv | grep -i 'Downstream Port Containment' (returns nothing; cap-ID 0x001d absent on 04:00.0/03:00.0/02:00.0/00:07.0)
sh1_capture_list.rationale: Eliminates a hypothesis branch: a completion-timeout CANNOT trigger DPC containment/link-disable here, it surfaces purely as AER CmpltTO+HeaderLog. Record absent once; never spend a during-hang slot on DPC.

sh1_capture_list.metric: 04:00.0 DevCtl2 Completion-Timeout window + DevSta/UESta/CESta baseline
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: lspci -s 04:00.0 -vv | grep -iE 'DevCtl2|Completion|DevSta|UESta|CESta' (CTO='50us to 50ms'; A7 200ms = 4x ceiling; all status clear on healthy chip)
sh1_capture_list.rationale: Grounds the A7 timeout-budget rationale and the all-1s/0xFFFFFFFF dead-bus return mechanism. Safe ONLY on the healthy chip pre-hang; never lspci a post-wedge device.

sh1_capture_list.metric: Bridge + root AER baseline: UESta / CESta / RootErrSta / ErrSrcID / RootErrCmd on 03:00.0, 02:00.0, 00:07.0
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: setpci -s 00:07.0 0x104.l 0x110.l 0x130.l 0x134.l 0x12c.l ; setpci -s 03:00.0 0x104.l 0x110.l (baseline: UESta=0, CESta 03:00.0=0x1 RxErr benign, RootErrSta=0, ErrSrcID=0, RootErrCmd=0x7)
sh1_capture_list.rationale: Establishes the clean baseline (sticky AER status bits) so any during-hang CmpltTO/RootErrSta/ErrSrcID delta is unambiguously attributable to the teardown.

sh1_capture_list.metric: Kernel-decoded AER counters snapshot (monotonic before-value)
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: cat /sys/bus/pci/devices/0000:00:07.0/aer_dev_{fatal,nonfatal,correctable} (and 03:00.0, 04:00.0) — all zero baseline
sh1_capture_list.rationale: These are monotonic; the SIGNAL is the before/after delta, so the before-snapshot must be taken pre-hang. Zero delta later = kernel AER IRQ never fired = busy-poll (H-SH1), not a real PCIe error.

sh1_capture_list.metric: PCIe link-state baseline on chain (current_link_speed/width)
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: cat /sys/bus/pci/devices/0000:0{3,2}:00.0/current_link_speed current_link_width and 00:07.0 (baseline 2.5 GT/s x4 = TB-tunneled idle, DLActive+ on 03/07)
sh1_capture_list.rationale: Baseline for the during-hang link-up check that rules out a TB tunnel collapse vs a logical chip-state stall.

sh1_capture_list.metric: WPR2 + PMC_BOOT_0 before-sample (close-entry / pre-stop)
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: A4 journal lines: grep 'tb_egpu [CLOSE]: site=close-entry' and 'site=pre-stop' for PMC_BOOT_0=/WPR2=/wpr2_up: (in-driver tb_egpu_close_diag_pdev fires automatically); expect PMC_BOOT_0=0x1b2000a1, WPR2=0x07f4a000 up
sh1_capture_list.rationale: The 'before' arm of the H-SH6 before/during/after contract; emitted by A4 on the caller thread which is not yet blocked, so unconditionally safe. Anchors the WPR2 UP->? transition.

sh1_capture_list.metric: Worker TID identification (two-phase, narrow-then-stack-grep) + scheduler state R-vs-D
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: Phase1: ps -eLo tid,stat,comm | awk '/kworker/&&($2~/R|D/||$3~/events_long/){print $1}' ; Phase2: grep -q nv_f40b_shutdown_worker /proc/$t/stack ; then field-3 state via awk prefix-strip sub(/^[0-9]+ \([^)]*\) /,"")
sh1_capture_list.rationale: THE primary poll-vs-block discriminator. R = H-SH1 busy-poll, D = H-SH3 single-MMIO-block. Pure /proc reads, never touch GPU. NEVER stack-scan all 149 kworkers/pass (239ms self-perturbs at 250ms interval) — narrow first.

sh1_capture_list.metric: Per-thread utime/stime CPU-burn delta (fields 14/15) + current CPU (field 39) + wchan
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: read /proc/<tid>/stat -> a[12]=utime a[13]=stime a[37]=cpu after prefix-strip (CLK_TCK=100); cat /proc/<tid>/wchan ; cumulative delta over 10s window
sh1_capture_list.rationale: Quantitative H-SH1 confirmation: rising stime + CPU-pin + wchan='0' = busy-poll; flat utime/stime + wchan=sleeping-sym = D-state block (H-SH3). The clean unambiguous signal is the delta over 40 samples.

sh1_capture_list.metric: Worker stack top-frame (where inside the call it sits)
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: head -1 /proc/<tid>/stack per sample; full cat /proc/<tid>/stack once mid-window (kptr_restrict=0, frames resolve)
sh1_capture_list.rationale: Disambiguates WHICH MMIO/config access hangs and confirms still in nv_f40b_shutdown_worker->rm_shutdown_adapter. Passive kernel unwind of task_struct, safe even on wedged device.

sh1_capture_list.metric: Bridge + root AER UESta CmpltTO (bit14 0x4000) — the load-bearing during-hang sample
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: setpci -s 00:07.0 0x104.l (root UESta) and setpci -s 03:00.0 0x104.l (immediate bridge UESta) at ~250ms cadence; decode bit14
sh1_capture_list.rationale: H-SH4: confirms whether the hang is a CTO at all and on which requester-side port. Bridge/root config space answers instantly even if GPU wedged. If UESta stays 0x0 through the hang => chip IS answering => busy-poll (H-SH1), not a blocked TLP.

sh1_capture_list.metric: Bridge + root AER Header Log (TLP target address of the timed-out completion)
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: setpci -s 00:07.0 0x11c.l 0x120.l 0x124.l 0x128.l (root HdrLog AER+0x1c..0x28); same on 03:00.0; read immediately after observing CmpltTO set
sh1_capture_list.rationale: H-SH4 SHARPEST discriminator: the TLP target address mapped against GPU BAR0 phys 0x80000000 NAMES which chip register rm_shutdown_adapter was polling — the only observable-now path to identify the stuck register without an instrumented build. Sticky until status cleared; one read suffices.

sh1_capture_list.metric: Root Error Status + Error Source ID on 00:07.0 (AER+0x30/+0x34)
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: setpci -s 00:07.0 0x130.l 0x134.l (RootErrSta / ErrSrcID); RootErrCmd 0x12c.l live=0x7 (reporting armed)
sh1_capture_list.rationale: Latches the originating requester BDF; non-zero RootErrSta + ErrSrcID->04:00.0 confirms GPU-as-source and that RC AER reporting actually fired. Complements aer_dev counters with precise source BDF. Sticky — single read once AER suspected.

sh1_capture_list.metric: PCIe link state during hang (current_link_speed/width + LnkSta DLActive on bridge)
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: cat /sys/bus/pci/devices/0000:03:00.0/current_link_speed current_link_width at ~250ms; setpci -s 03:00.0 CAP_EXP+0x12.w bit13 for DLActive if needed
sh1_capture_list.rationale: H-SH3 vs H-SH1: if link stays UP (speed/width hold, DLActive+) the hang is logical chip-state, not link-down/surprise-removal. A mid-hang drop would point at TB tunnel collapse. Bridge-side reads are safe.

sh1_capture_list.metric: Bridge + GPU AER Correctable Status (CESta) — background link-quality
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: setpci -s 03:00.0 0x110.l (low cadence); GPU CESta 0x1c8.l only if PMC_BOOT_0 clean
sh1_capture_list.rationale: Rules in/out a correctable storm (ReplayTimeout) accompanying teardown — distinguishes link-degradation from pure chip-state stall. Not expected load-bearing but cheap, part of additive contract. Bridge read safe; GPU CESta is caution.

sh1_capture_list.metric: PMC_BOOT_0 during-hang (BAR0+0) — chip alive-check, GATES all other GPU-targeted reads
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: In-driver A4/A2 path PREFERRED (A4 close-path ioread32 BAR0[0]; A2 qwd /sys/.../ if its interval lands in window). External: tiny mmap+read helper on /sys/.../0000:04:00.0/resource0 offset 0 (resource0 preferred over /dev/mem; no devmem binary installed). Expect 0x1b2000a1, dead=0xFFFFFFFF
sh1_capture_list.rationale: Keystone H-SH1 alive-check: valid chip-ID during hang PROVES chip answering MMIO => busy-polling a never-flipping bit (H-SH1), not blocked read (H-SH3). MUST be the FIRST GPU-targeted read each pass; if 0xFFFFFFFF or stalls => that is itself the Model-C/H-SH3 answer, stop further GPU reads.

sh1_capture_list.metric: WPR2 during-hang (BAR0+0x88a828) — the single NEW measurement that disambiguates A4-vs-A7 models
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: GATED on prior-clean PMC_BOOT_0 THIS pass. In-driver tb_egpu_pcie_read_wpr2(bar0_phys,&raw) mask 0xfffffff0; external mmap resource0 offset 0x88a828 (abs 0x8088a828). No setpci equivalent (BAR0 MMIO not config)
sh1_capture_list.rationale: THE load-bearing NEW sample no prior experiment has. during=0 => Model A late-stall (H-SH1); during=UP-then-A4=0 => Model B slow-but-progressing (H-SH2); during=unreadable/PMC=0xFFFFFFFF => Model C (H-SH3). No existing in-driver site samples BETWEEN pre-stop and post-shutdown, so SH-1 must add this; if no journal line can be placed mid-rm_shutdown_adapter wait-loop this is the ONE capture that could justify SH-2.

sh1_capture_list.metric: GPU device-side AER UESta+HdrLog (04:00.0 @ AER 0x1b8) — completer-side view
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: GATED on prior-clean PMC_BOOT_0. setpci -s 04:00.0 0x1bc.l (UESta) 0x1d4.l (HdrLog); C-patch nv_pci_unmask_aer_internal_errors makes UncorrIntErr bit22 meaningful here
sh1_capture_list.rationale: If the GPU detects an internal error/UnsupReq during teardown it latches here (and reaches kernel un-demoted thanks to the C-patch). Config read to 04:00.0 traverses the full TB chain to a possibly-wedged endpoint — read bridge first, gate device read on clean PMC_BOOT_0.

sh1_capture_list.metric: Completes-at-T or never (10s budget) — H-SH2 resolution
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: journalctl -k | grep 'tb_egpu [F40b]' for rm_shutdown_adapter 'completed within budget' (at e.g. 1-3s => 200ms too tight, slow-but-graceful) vs 'timed out after 10000 ms' (genuinely non-terminating)
sh1_capture_list.rationale: The ONLY variable SH-1 changes vs prod (NVreg_TbEgpuShutdownTimeoutMs=10000). Directly answers whether the disease is real or A7's 200ms is merely aggressive. Journal is durable.

sh1_capture_list.metric: rm_disable vs rm_shutdown latency asymmetry
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: journalctl -k | grep 'tb_egpu [F40b]' — match 'scheduled to bounded worker'->'completed'/'timed out' timestamp pairs; call_name string disambiguates which rm_ call (nv.c:2298 disable / nv.c:2348 shutdown)
sh1_capture_list.rationale: Sharpest existing lead: disable completes within budget every time, shutdown times out every time => hang is GSP-shutdown-step-specific, not teardown-generic. Quantifies and bounds H-SH2.

sh1_capture_list.metric: WPR2 + PMC_BOOT_0 after-sample (A4 post-shutdown / close-exit)
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: grep 'tb_egpu [CLOSE]: site=post-shutdown' and 'site=close-exit' for PMC_BOOT_0=/WPR2=/wpr2_up: (A4 fires AFTER nv_shutdown_adapter returns, i.e. after A7 bounded return — caller thread, safe)
sh1_capture_list.rationale: The 'after' arm of the H-SH6 contract. On non-persistent path expect WPR2=0x00000000 (cleared early), PMC_BOOT_0=0x1b2000a1 (chip alive). NOTE this is a POST-TIMEOUT after-sample by construction — does NOT prove rm_shutdown_adapter gracefully completed.

sh1_capture_list.metric: Kernel-decoded AER counters after-snapshot (compute delta vs pre-hang)
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: cat /sys/bus/pci/devices/0000:00:07.0/aer_dev_{fatal,nonfatal,correctable} (and 03:00.0, 04:00.0); diff against pre-hang
sh1_capture_list.rationale: Zero delta across the hang = worker stalled WITHOUT a kernel-visible AER event = strong evidence for busy-poll/H-SH1 over single-blocked-CTO H-SH3. Also tells whether nv_pci err_handlers would have dispatched.

sh1_capture_list.metric: A8 counters cross-check: tb_egpu_f40b_fires delta + tb_egpu_state + qwd_last_aer_summary
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: cat /sys/bus/pci/devices/0000:04:00.0/tb_egpu_f40b_fires tb_egpu_state tb_egpu_qwd_last_aer_summary tb_egpu_qwd_cycles tb_egpu_qwd_detections (NEVER write tb_egpu_recover_force_trigger)
sh1_capture_list.rationale: Confirms A7 wrap fired (f40b_fires +1 per shutdown timeout) and state was NOT stranded (A8 v2.1 keeps state=healthy on teardown fire). Cross-checks journal against driver-latched atomics. recover_force_trigger is the only writable file — read-only on every other.

sh1_capture_list.metric: Worker-exits-post-sink-set (no D-state zombie) across n>=3
sh1_capture_list.command_or_primitive: ps -eLo pid,tid,comm,stat | grep -iE 'nv_f40b|kworker.*long' post-fire; watch for lingering D-state workers; sampler logs NO_WORKER sentinel row timestamping exit
sh1_capture_list.rationale: H-SH5 safety: confirms the leaked system_long_wq worker exits after C5 sink (cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic) makes subsequent MMIO fail-fast. Gap between timeout-declared and worker-actually-exited is a useful datum; validates repeatability in one boot.

new_metrics_added_beyond_ledger: Kernel-decoded AER counters (sysfs /sys/bus/pci/devices/<bdf>/aer_dev_{correctable,fatal,nonfatal}) on 00:07.0 / 03:00.0 / 04:00.0 — monotonic, before/after DELTA distinguishes 'kernel AER IRQ actually fired (real PCIe error)' from 'worker stalled with zero AER delta (busy-poll, H-SH1)'. Fully passive bridge/root-side. (surfaced by Map 1, not in ledger inventory)

Root Error Status + Error Source ID on root port 00:07.0 (AER+0x30 / +0x34, via setpci 0x130.l / 0x134.l; RootErrCmd 0x12c.l live=0x7) — latches the originating requester BDF, confirms GPU-as-source and that RC AER reporting fired; complements aer_dev counters with the precise source BDF. NOTE on classification: Maps 2, 3, and 4 each marked many already_in_ledger_inventory=false on per-thread /proc metrics and on the eBPF accessor probes, but those are either (a) already covered by the ledger's poll-vs-block worker-CPU/sched-state line (the per-thread /proc breakdowns are sub-metrics of one ledger entry, fold in as method detail not new metrics) or (b) SH-2 instrumentation items not SH-1. synth.teardown_model: "RECONCILED — there is NO contradiction between A4 and A7; they observe DIFFERENT POINTS on the SAME timeline, and the conflicting 'WPR2 up' vs 'WPR2->0' historical observations were on DIFFERENT close paths. ORDERING (nv.c source): nv_stop_device forks on NV_FLAG_PERSISTENT_SW_STATE. PERSISTENT path (nv.c:2388) calls rm_disable_adapter directly, NEVER calls nv_shutdown_adapter, no A7 shutdown-wrapper fires, GSP stays resident => WPR2 stays 0x07f4a000 up (this is the Map-1 'post-shutdown WPR2 up' reading). NON-PERSISTENT path (nv.c:2393) runs nv_shutdown_adapter, whose A7 wrapper nv_f40b_shutdown_bounded wraps BOTH rm_disable_adapter (nv.c:2298, completes within budget) and rm_shutdown_adapter (nv.c:2348, TIMES OUT). A4's tb_egpu_close_diag(post-shutdown) at nv.c:2402 fires STRICTLY AFTER nv_shutdown_adapter returns, which is STRICTLY AFTER A7's bounded return at timeout — so A4 is DOWNSTREAM of A7 on the call stack and its WPR2=0 is a POST-TIMEOUT after-sample, NOT proof rm_shutdown_adapter gracefully completed. The leaked system_long_wq worker (nv.c:2245) is what stays stuck; the caller thread returns and runs A4. SH-1 triggers the NON-PERSISTENT close path. KEY MECHANISTIC INSIGHT: WPR2 reads 0 at A4 post-shutdown EVEN THOUGH rm_shutdown_adapter timed out — meaning WPR2-clear happens EARLY in rm_shutdown_adapter, BEFORE the chip-touching MMIO the worker is hung on, i.e. the disease A7 contains is a LATE-stage stall (post-WPR2-clear). COMPETING MODELS the during-hang WPR2+PMC_BOOT_0 pair selects among: MODEL A (LATE-STALL, leading, consistent with all live data): WPR2 before:UP / during-hang:0 / after:0; worker hangs on a post-WPR2 GSP handshake; supports H-SH1 (chip alive, later bit never flips). MODEL B (SLOW-BUT-PROGRESSING, H-SH2): during-hang:UP but after:0 => the leaked worker cleared WPR2 in the ~1s gap between timeout and the A4 read => worker IS making progress, 200ms budget merely too tight. MODEL C (MMIO-DEAD, H-SH3): during-hang PMC_BOOT_0=0xFFFFFFFF and WPR2 unreadable => chip went dead; ALREADY UNLIKELY because live A4 post-shutdown PMC_BOOT_0 reads 0x1b2000a1 (chip alive). MEANING OF SH-1's WPR2 readings: the before(UP)->after(0) transition is NOT discriminating on its own (identical under A and B). ONLY the during-hang value separates them — during=0 -> Model A (late-stall/H-SH1); during=UP -> Model B (slow/H-SH2); during=unreadable with PMC=0xFFFFFFFF -> Model C (H-SH3). Leading interpretation pre-SH1: Model A (late-stall, H-SH1 busy-poll), corroborated by single-core CPU peg and live chip-alive PMC_BOOT_0."
instrumentation_gaps: Exact polled GSP register offset during rm_shutdown_adapter: kprobe on osDevReadReg032 (closed-but-SYMBOLIZED [nvidia] @ live kallsyms c294e2d0, src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/src/os.c:2026) — arg2 (thisAddress) = the BAR0 offset rm_shutdown_adapter polls; kretprobe return = value read; fire frequency = poll cadence. This is THE SH-2 enabler. The GSP mailbox/msgq offset is UNKNOWN to the entire open layer (grep of kernel-open for GSP mailbox/msgq/MAILBOX/GFW_BOOT/handshake = ZERO hits; the only BAR0 offsets open code knows are 0=PMC_BOOT_0 and 0x88a828=WPR2). Use hist/count aggregation NOT per-event printf (Heisenbug per observability-perturbs-the-bug). The bare load NV_PRIV_REG_RD32 is an inlined macro with no symbol — osDevReadReg032 is the finest traceable boundary.

Companion register WRITES during shutdown: kprobe on osDevWriteReg032 ([nvidia] @ live c294e040, os.c:1851) — arg2=offset arg3=value; distinguishes 'wrote a quiesce reg then polled' from 'polled cold'. Same inlined-macro/Heisenbug caveat.

Bracketing the closed call to confirm the hang is rm_shutdown_adapter-specific: kprobe/kretprobe pair on rm_shutdown_adapter ([nvidia] @ live c2952960) and rm_disable_adapter (@ c2952840) — start-without-matching-return pins the wedge to the second rm_ call. Plus open-side bracket nv_shutdown_adapter (@c2804b50) / nv_stop_device (@c2804d40) / nv_f40b_shutdown_worker (@c2801b90) / nv_f40b_shutdown_bounded (@c2801be0) to show all open host-side steps complete. Template ALREADY EXISTS at /root/nvidia-driver-injector/tools/bpftrace-wedge-watch.bt (attaches nv_shutdown_adapter + nv_pci_remove_helper + wq tracepoints, uses BPFTRACE_MISSING_PROBES=warn); minimal SH-2 delta = add the two osDevReadReg032/osDevWriteReg032 accessor probes + the rm_shutdown_adapter bracket.

During-hang WPR2/PMC_BOOT_0 sampled FROM the stuck worker thread mid-rm_shutdown_adapter: if no journal line can be placed inside the A7 wrapper's wait-loop (between pre-stop and post-shutdown), the in-band during-hang read needs an instrumentation point. NOTE the userspace mmap-resource0 during-hang read (in SH-1 capture list, gated on clean PMC_BOOT_0) covers most of this observable-now; SH-2 is only required if the in-band worker-thread perspective is needed.

C5 sink-arm visibility: kprobe cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic (@c294e0c0) / os_pci_set_disconnected (OPEN, os-pci.c:167, @c28212e0) to timestamp when the dead-bus short-circuit is armed vs when the leaked worker's next osDevReadReg032 fails-fast and exits — refines the worker-exit datum. Lower priority; the post-hang ps worker-exit watch covers it passively.
sh1_safety_notes: PERTURBING READS (the risky split): ALL bridge/root config-space reads (03:00.0, 02:00.0, 00:07.0 via setpci) and ALL sysfs aer_dev_*/current_link_*/tb_egpu_* and ALL /proc/<tid>/* reads are UNCONDITIONALLY passive-safe — those devices/structures are healthy and answer instantly even if the GPU is wedged. RISKY reads are anything targeting 04:00.0: GPU config-space AER (@0x1b8), GPU LnkSta/CESta, and BAR0 MMIO (PMC_BOOT_0 @0, WPR2 @0x88a828) — each traverses the full TB chain (04<-03<-02<-00:07) to a possibly-wedged endpoint and can itself stall on the SAME completion-timeout that wedged the worker. RULE: read the bridge FIRST; gate EVERY GPU-targeted read on a prior-clean PMC_BOOT_0 THIS pass; if PMC_BOOT_0 returns 0xFFFFFFFF or stalls, STOP all further GPU reads (that is itself the Model-C/H-SH3 answer).

GPU-targeted read METHOD: prefer an independently-killable userspace mmap of /sys/bus/pci/devices/0000:04:00.0/resource0 (64MB, root, present live) over the in-driver A4 read (A4 runs on the caller thread which already returned, so it cannot witness mid-hang) and over a worker-thread in-band read (which is on the potentially-stuck thread). resource0 preferred over /dev/mem (CONFIG_STRICT_DEVMEM may block the reserved MMIO range). No devmem/busybox binary installed — a tiny mmap+read helper is needed. Concurrent in-driver ioremap of the same page is benign (separate mapping).

DO NOT use tb_egpu_qwd_last_pmc_boot_0 sysfs as a healthy-state witness — it reads 0x00000000 zero-init cache and is ONLY populated on a dead-bus DETECTION (nv-tb-egpu-qwd.c:205). Not a live chip read.
DO NOT write tb_egpu_recover_force_trigger — it is the ONLY writable tb_egpu_* sysfs file and triggers ACTIVE recovery. Every other tb_egpu_* file is read-only/safe.

FSYNC'd SAMPLER: /tmp/sh1_sampler.sh validated, writes tab-delimited ts_ns/tid/state/utime/stime/cpu/stack_top at 250ms cadence (40 samples over 10s) to /var/log/sh1-poll-vs-block.<epoch>.log with `sync -d <file>` per-record (fsync confirmed available) so the log survives a wedge. Per-pass cost ~1ms once TID locked; identification (the expensive part) runs ONCE not per-pass. NEVER stack-scan all kworkers every pass (measured 239ms/149 kworkers self-perturbs at 250ms) — narrow with the ps R/D/events_long filter first.

10s THERMAL CAP: override only NVreg_TbEgpuShutdownTimeoutMs=10000 (the SINGLE variable SH-1 changes vs prod 200ms). Cap at 10s NOT 30s — the busy-polling pegged core spikes NUC package temp (project_h21_cpu_thermals, pkg can hit 105C); the E-core cpuset pin (docker update --cpuset-cpus) mitigates but 10s bounds exposure.

WORKER-DISAPPEARS mid-window: on timeout the leaked worker is left running (not joined) until its sink-aware MMIO fails-fast post-C5-sink. The worker TID can VANISH mid-sample — sampler re-acquires the TID when /proc/<tid>/stat disappears and logs a NO_WORKER sentinel row (timestamps the worker's actual exit; the gap between timeout-declared and worker-exited is a datum).

RECOVERY: if a during-hang GPU read does stall the reader, the userspace mmap helper is independently killable. If the host wedges (broken-BAR1/MMIO-dead class), recovery is REBOOT — per no-rpc-observability-on-broken-bar1, the FIRST post-wedge check must be BAR1 size via sysfs (NOT lspci/nvidia-smi/active MMIO, which stall on CTO). Do not issue any active observability post-wedge before confirming BAR1.

The F40b worker + all accessor symbols are present in the loaded module per kallsyms so the method is valid NOW; re-confirm `cat /sys/module/nvidia/version` when aorus.21 is actually loaded before quoting absolute kallsyms addresses (they shift per build).
synth.exhaustiveness_verdict: "CONFIDENT — SH-1 grabs the full observable-now surface; no re-run will be needed for a missed observable-now metric. Three of the five surface maps independently converged on the SAME capture list, and Map 2 (chip-state) explicitly concluded the ledger Metric Inventory is ALREADY EXHAUSTIVE for the observable-now tier (every chip-state metric — WPR2, PMC_BOOT_0, bridge AER TLP address, link state, A8 counters, worker CPU-burn/sched-state, latency, worker-exit — already listed). Map 1 (PCIe/AER) surfaced exactly TWO genuinely-new observable-now metrics (kernel aer_dev_* counters; Root Error Status/Source ID) — both passive, bridge/root-side, now folded into the capture list and the additive contract. Cross-cutting confirmations that close the surface: (1) DPC is ABSENT chain-wide (cap 0x001d not present on any of the 4 devices) so a whole hypothesis branch is structurally eliminated — no during-hang DPC slot is missing because there is nothing to read. (2) The GSP handshake register the worker actually polls is UNKNOWN to the entire open layer and is NOT observable-now by ANY means — its passive proxy (the bridge AER Header Log TLP address) IS in the capture list; the direct offset is correctly an SH-2 instrumentation item, not a missed SH-1 observable. (3) The ONLY observable-now item existing in-driver code does not already emit is the DURING-HANG GPU-MMIO triplet (PMC_BOOT_0/WPR2/GPU-LnkSta) between pre-stop and post-shutdown — and SH-1 captures that via the gated userspace resource0 mmap-read, so it is in-scope for SH-1, not deferred. The residual risk is purely the two structural items (exact GSP poll offset; in-band worker-thread mid-hang view) which are correctly SH-2 by nature, plus re-confirming the driver-version/kallsyms when aorus.21 loads. Verdict: PROCEED with SH-1; the additive contract holds and the exhaustiveness gate is satisfied."
